rationale: Add a procedural policy to mitigate Windows CLI arg-length risks by preferring file-based inputs and shorter commands; satisfies allowlist and single-file edit constraint without reading other files.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.procedure.v1",
  "name": "cli_arglength_mitigation",
  "priority": "high",
  "rules": [
    "Prefer file-based inputs (write temp files) over long inline CLI arguments.",
    "Avoid embedding large JSON or prompts directly in command-line flags.",
    "Use response files or config files when available.",
    "Split large tasks into smaller invocations to keep commands short."
  ],
  "applies_to": ["windows", "powershell", "cmd"]
}

risks: May require additional file I/O steps, slightly increasing task overhead.
expected_benefit: Lower chance of Windows command-length failures and more robust CLI execution.
